brief_document.title: Fix CLI run_id resolution and list request construction
brief_document.purpose: Every CLI command except 'start' is broken. 'list' returns 'invalid argument', and describe/signal/cancel/query all fail with 'run id is missing'. The CLI passes None for run_id but the server rejects requests without one. The CLI should resolve the latest run automatically — a developer should never need to know or provide a run_id for basic operations.
brief_document.task: Fix the CLI so that describe, signal, cancel, and query commands work with just a workflow_id by auto-resolving the latest run_id from the server. Fix the list command's request construction. If the server requires a run_id, the client should fetch it first via describe or list, then use it. If the server can be changed to accept None as 'latest run', that's even better.

requirements.title: CLI commands work with workflow_id alone
requirements.spec: WHEN a developer runs 'aion describe <workflow_id>' without specifying a run_id, THE SYSTEM SHALL resolve the latest run and return the description. The same applies to signal, cancel, and query. THE SYSTEM SHALL accept an optional --run-id flag for cases where a specific run is needed.
acceptance: 'aion list' returns workflow summaries without error.
'aion describe <workflow_id>' returns the workflow description with history.
'aion signal <workflow_id> <name> --payload {}' succeeds against a running workflow.
'aion cancel <workflow_id>' succeeds against a running workflow.
An optional --run-id flag is accepted by describe/signal/cancel/query.

boundaries: SHALL NOT change the gRPC protocol definitions.
MAY change server handler code if needed to accept None as 'use latest run'.
SHALL NOT require the developer to look up or provide run_id for basic operations.
verification: The hello-world tutorial can be completed end-to-end using only aion-cli commands.
